2606.31366.title: From Materials Database to Materials Bank: Assetizing Data for AI Driven Materials Innovation
2606.31366.paper_summary: Driven by high-throughput experimentation, computational modeling, and artificial intelligence (AI), materials data has expanded at an unprecedented rate. Conventional materials databases function only as passive repositories, archiving raw experimental records indiscriminately including both successful and failed data, without systematic value filtering or asset management. This creates a critical gap between massive data accumulation and actionable innovation, hindering the identification of high-potential materials and industrial translation. To address this bottleneck, we propose an industrialization-oriented Materials Bank, a dedicated valuefiltering and assetization layer that operates beyond traditional databases. It does not merely curate high-quality data but systematically elevates qualified candidates into standardized, upgradable materials assets via a multi-dimensional BankCard framework covering scientific validity, synthesis feasibility, application readiness, and industrial value. By unifying databases, AI models, automated experimentation, and multi-criteria assessment into a cohesive closed-loop ecosystem, the Materials Bank establishes a clear trajectory from data to knowledge, candidate, asset, and product. It serves not as an enhanced database or screening tool, but as a decision infrastructure bridging academic discovery and industrial demand, offering a scalable paradigm to accelerate AI-driven materials innovation and deliver tangible real-world impact.
2606.31366.paper_summary_zh: 受到高通量實驗、計算建模和人工智慧（AI）的驅動，材料數據以空前的速度擴展。傳統的材料數據庫僅作為被動的資料庫，隨意存檔原始實驗記錄，包括成功和失敗的數據，沒有系統的價值篩選或資產管理。這在大量數據積累和可行的創新之間造成了關鍵的鴻溝，阻礙了高潛力材料的識別和工業轉化。為了解決這一瓶頸，我們提出了一個以工業化為導向的材料銀行，這是一個專門的價值篩選和資產化層，運作超越傳統數據庫。它不僅僅是策劃高質量數據，而是通過一個涵蓋科學有效性、合成可行性、應用準備性和工業價值的多維BankCard框架，系統性地將合格候選者提升為標準化、可升級的材料資產。通過將數據庫、AI模型、自動化實驗和多標準評估統一成一個有機的閉環生態系統，材料銀行建立了一條從數據到知識、候選者、資產和產品的清晰軌跡。它不僅僅是增強的數據庫或篩選工具，而是一個決策基礎設施，橋接學術發現和工業需求，提供一個可擴展的範式，以加速基於AI的材料創新並實現可觸及的現實影響。
2606.31366.author: Chenyao Ma et.al.
2606.31366.authors: Chenyao Ma, Di Zhang, Weibo Gong, Wei Du, Rui Su, Yuhang Chen, Kan Xu, Huan Gu, Limin Li, Piao Ma, Zhenghao Li, Hao Li
